sections.name: Single Sign-On
sections.description: Implement single sign-on using modern and industry standard protocols

sections.name: HTTPS-only - Traffic Redirects
sections.description:
Redirect traffic from HTTP protocol (port 80) to HTTPS (port 443)

```
This does not apply to secure protocols designed to run on top of unencrypted connections, such as OCSP
```

sections.name: HTTPS-only - TLS Testing
sections.description: Produce a clear scan using a widely adopted TLS scanning tool such as [ssllabs](https://www.ssllabs.com/)

sections.name: HTTPS-only - Transport Security
sections.description: Include the Strict-Transport-Security header on all pages with the includeSubdomains directive

sections.name: Content Security Policy
sections.description: Set a minimally permissive Content Security Policy

sections.name: Password Policy
sections.description:
If password authentication is used in addition to single sign-on:
- Do not limit the permitted characters that can be used
- Do not limit the length of the password to anything below 64 characters
- Do not use secret questions (place of birth, etc) as a sole password reset requirement
- Require email verification of a password change request
- Require the current password in addition to the new password during password change
- Verify newly created passwords against common passwords lists or leaked passwords databases
- Check existing user passwords for compromise regularly
- Store passwords in a hashed and salted format using a memory-hard or CPU-hard one-way hash function
- Enforce appropriate account lockout and brute-force protection on account access

sections.name: Security Libraries
sections.description:
Use frameworks, template languages, or libraries that systemically address implementation weaknesses by escaping the outputs and sanitizing the inputs.

```
Example: ORM for database access, UI framework for rendering DOM
```

sections.name: Dependency Patching
sections.description: Apply security patches with a severity score of "medium" or higher, or ensure equivalent mitigations are available for all components of the application stack within one month of the patch release

sections.name: Logging - User Access
sections.description: Log user log-in and log-out

sections.name: Logging - CRUD Operations
sections.description: Log CRUD (create, read, update, delete) operations on application and system users and objects

sections.name: Logging - Security Settings
sections.description: Log security settings changes (including disabling or modifying of logging)

sections.name: Logging - Access to Data
sections.description: Log application owner or administrator access to customer data (i.e. Access Transparency)

sections.name: Logging - Log Content
sections.description: Logs must include user ID, IP address, valid timestamp, type of action performed, and object of this action.

sections.name: Logging - Log Retention
sections.description: Logs must be stored for at least 30 days, and should not contain sensitive data or payloads

sections.name: Backup and Disaster recovery - Operation
sections.description: Securely back up all data to a different location than where the application is running (physical, logical, or availability region based, etc)

sections.name: Backup and Disaster recovery - Plan Testing
sections.description: Maintain and periodically test disaster recovery plans

sections.name: Backup and Disaster recovery - Restoration Testing
sections.description: Periodically test backup restoration

sections.name: Encryption
sections.description: Use available means of encryption to protect sensitive data in transit between systems and at rest in online data storages and backups

sections.name: List of Data
sections.description: Maintain a list of sensitive data types that the application is expected to process

sections.name: Data Flow Diagram
sections.description: Maintain an up-to-date diagram indicating how sensitive data reaches your systems and where it ends up being stored

sections.name: Vulnerability Prevention - Auth Bypass
sections.description:
Train your developers and implement development guidelines to prevent authorization bypass attacks. 

Example: Accessing other customers' data or admin features from a regular account

sections.name: Vulnerability Prevention - Insecure Session IDs
sections.description:
Train your developers and implement development guidelines to prevent use of insecure session IDs. 

Examples: Guessable token; a token stored in an insecure location (e.g. cookie without secure and httpOnly flags set)

sections.name: Vulnerability Prevention - Injection Attacks
sections.description:
Train your developers and implement development guidelines to prevent Injection attacks. 

Examples: SQL injection, NoSQL injection, XXE, OS command injection

sections.name: Vulnerability Prevention - XSS Attacks
sections.description:
Train your developers and implement development guidelines to prevent Cross-site scripting attacks. 

Examples: Calling insecure JavaScript functions, performing insecure DOM manipulations, echoing back user input into HTML without escaping

sections.name: Vulnerability Prevention - XSRF Attacks
sections.description:
Train your developers and implement development guidelines to prevent Cross-site request forgery attacks.

Example: Accepting requests with an Origin header from a different domain

sections.name: Vulnerability Prevention - Vulnerable Libraries
sections.description:
Train your developers and implement development guidelines to prevent the use of vulnerable libraries. 

Example: Using server-side frameworks or JavaScript libraries with known vulnerabilities

sections.name: Time to Fix Vulnerabilities
sections.description: Produce and deploy patches to address application vulnerabilities that materially impact security within 90 days of discovery.

sections.name: Vulnerability Reports - Point of Contact
sections.description: Publish the point of contact for security reports on your website

sections.name: Vulnerability Reports - Vulnerability Notice Response
sections.description: Respond to security reports within a reasonable time frame

sections.name: Customer Testing - Application Testing
sections.description: On request, enable your customers or their delegates to test the security of your application

sections.name: Customer Testing - No Production Testing
sections.description: Test on a non-production environment if it closely resembles the production environment in functionality

sections.name: Customer Testing - Production Data Separated
sections.description: Ensure non-production environments do not contain production data

sections.name: Self-assessment
sections.description: Perform annual (at a minimum) security self-assessments using this document

sections.name: External testing
sections.description: Contract a security vendor to perform annual, comprehensive penetration tests on your systems

sections.name: Training
sections.description: Implement role-specific security training for your personnel that is relevant to their business function

sections.name: Compliance - Industry Standards
sections.description: Comply with all industry security standards relevant to your business such as PCI DSS, HITRUST, ISO27001, and SSAE 18

sections.name: Compliance - Regulatory and Legal
sections.description: Comply with local laws and regulations in jurisdictions applicable to your company and your customers, such as GDPR, Binding Corporate Rules, and Standard Contractual Clauses

sections.name: Incident Handling - Notification Timeline
sections.description: Notify your customers about a breach without undue delay, no later than 72 hours upon discovery

sections.name: Incident Handling - Notification Details
sections.description:
Include the following information in customer notification of any breach:
- Relevant point of contact
- Preliminary technical analysis of the breach
- Remediation plan with reasonable timelines

sections.name: Data sanitization
sections.description: Ensure media sanitization processes based on NIST SP 800-88 or equivalent are implemented

sections.name: Physical Access - Perimeter and Interior
sections.description: Implement and validate layered physical perimeter controls and interior barriers

sections.name: Physical Access - Keys and Key Cards
sections.description: Implement and validate managed access to physical keys or keycards

sections.name: Physical Access - Logging and Records
sections.description: Implement and validate managed access to physical entry and exit logs and records

sections.name: Physical Access - Response Plan
sections.description: Implement and validate the presence of an appropriate response plan for physical intruder or break-in alerts

sections.name: Logical Access - Least Priviledge
sections.description: Limit sensitive data access exclusively to users with a legitimate need. The data owner must authorize such access

sections.name: Logical Access - Account De-Activation
sections.description: Deactivate redundant accounts and expired access grants in a timely manner

sections.name: Logical Access - Access Reviews
sections.description: Perform regular reviews of access to data to validate need to know

sections.name: Subprocessors - Published List
sections.description: Publish a list of third-party companies with access to customer data on your website

sections.name: Subprocessors - Right to Audit/Assess
sections.description: Assess third-party companies annually against this baseline
